tasks.description: Tool overlap and gap analysis — client-side computation: overlapping tools, unused tools, coverage gaps, total tool spend summary. Add 'Tool Analysis' section/toggle to tools page with 4 analysis cards.
acceptance_criteria: 'Tool Analysis' section on tools page accessible via button or tab toggle
'Overlapping Tools' card: lists steps served by 2+ tools with combined monthly cost for those tools
'Unused Tools' card: lists tools with zero step_tools assignments
'Coverage Gaps' card: lists steps with no tool assigned, sorted by frequency_per_month descending
Total tool spend summary: monthly total, annual total, breakdown by status (active/considering/cancelled)
Click step name → navigate to step's canvas tab (requires section_id → tab lookup)
Click tool name → select that tool on the tools canvas (or scroll to it)
All data computed client-side from tools + steps + step_tools arrays — no new API endpoints needed

patterns_to_follow: page.tsx server component fetches data via Supabase, passes to client view component
Analysis cards use bg-surface-secondary rounded-md p-4 with white/90 headers and white/55 body text
Step/tool navigation: useRouter().push(`/w/${workspaceId}/${tabId}`) for steps, tool selection via local state for tools
Existing tools page.tsx fetches tools + toolSections — add steps + step_tools queries in parallel Promise.all
step_tools has no workspace_id column — fetch via .in('tool_id', toolIds) where toolIds come from already-fetched tools
Steps need: id, name, section_id, frequency_per_month — section_id used to find tab via sections table
learnings_relevant: apiFetch unwraps envelope — returns T directly
Server/API query mismatch — update both page.tsx and any API routes if adding joins

tasks.special_instructions: Data fetching approach: page.tsx should add two more queries to the existing Promise.all — (1) steps with select('id, name, section_id, frequency_per_month') filtered by workspace_id, (2) step_tools via .in('tool_id', toolIds) after tools are fetched (may need sequential instead of parallel for this one, or use a Supabase inner join: step_tools.select('id, step_id, tool_id, steps!inner(workspace_id)').eq('steps.workspace_id', workspaceId)). Pass steps and stepTools as new props to ToolsCanvasView. Create tool-analysis-view.tsx as a separate component rendered conditionally via a toggle button (e.g. BarChart3 icon). For step navigation, also fetch sections (id, tab_id) to map section_id → tab_id. Total spend summary should match the existing sidebar summary pattern in tools-canvas-view.tsx.

tasks.description: Add 'Go to Tools' link in step-detail-panel Assigned Tools section when workspace has no tools
acceptance_criteria: When no tools exist in workspace, Assigned Tools section shows 'No tools yet' with link to /w/{workspaceId}/tools
Link uses router.push or Next.js Link component
When tools exist but none assigned to step, shows existing empty dropdown behavior (no change)

patterns_to_follow: Existing 'Go to Canvas' link pattern in improvements-view.tsx
text-accent-blue hover:underline for internal navigation links

tasks.special_instructions: step-detail-panel.tsx already fetches tools for the dropdown (fetchTools). When the tools list is empty (length 0), show a text like 'No tools defined yet.' with a link 'Go to Tools →' that navigates to /w/{workspaceId}/tools. Use useWorkspace() or useParams() for workspaceId.

tasks.description: Compare view CTA should create both process and journey tabs, not just journey
acceptance_criteria: Compare view 'Go to Canvas' CTA creates the correct tab type based on context or offers both options
If comparison is between perspectives on process entities, creates process tab
If comparison is between perspectives on journey entities, creates journey tab
Navigation goes to the newly created tab

patterns_to_follow: Existing CTA button style: bg-accent-blue text-white rounded-md px-4 py-2

tasks.special_instructions: Read the current CTA implementation in compare-view.tsx (added in IMP-072, iteration 117). It likely calls createTab with canvas_type 'journey' only. Fix: if workspace already has tabs, navigate to an existing one. If creating new, check whether the comparison data contains process entities (steps/sections) or journey entities (touchpoints/stages) and create the appropriate tab type. If both, default to process tab or show two buttons.

shared_files.note: No shared file modifications needed. All 3 tasks touch non-overlapping files.
